goldens.input: How to place stop loss order

You can place a Stop-Loss order for your position by following these steps:

Select the script for which you want to set the Stop-Loss.
Specify the quantity, Stop-Loss price, and trigger price.
Ensure the trigger price is close to the Stop-Loss price for effective execution.
Swipe to sell or purchase as required.

Specify the quantity, Stop-Loss price, and trigger price.
Ensure the trigger price is close to the Stop-Loss price for effective execution.
Swipe to sell or purchase as required.

goldens.input: How to apply for  a buyback
goldens.expected_output:
You can apply for the Buyback through the Choice FinX Mobile Application or the Web Platform.
Through the Choice FinX Mobile Application:

Login to the Choice FinX application.
Go to the Portfolio section.
If you are eligible for the buyback and hold the respective stock, you will see a Buyback Banner below the portfolio valuation details.
Click on the banner and proceed with the buyback application as per the on-screen instructions.
Enter the quantity you wish to tender and submit your request.
Through the Choice FinX Web Platform:
1. Open the Choice FinX website:
https://finx.choiceindia.com/
2. Login using your registered Mobile Number/User ID and Password.
3. After successful login, you will be redirected to the Home Page.
4. Under the Smart Investments section, click on Buyback.
5. You will be able to view the list of stocks with active buyback opportunities.
6. Select the eligible stock and proceed with the application as per the on-screen instructions.

Important Points:

DDPI (Demat Debit and Pledge Instruction) must be active in your account to submit a buyback request.
If DDPI is not active, you can activate it by navigating to the Profile section of the FinX platform and completing the DDPI activation process.
The shares intended for buyback must be available in your Demat account and should not be pledged, frozen, or under any restriction at the time of application.
Buyback eligibility is available only to clients who held the shares on or before the specified record date.
If the stock is not visible under the Buyback section, it may indicate that the holdings do not meet the eligibility criteria.
On the last day of the buyback tendering period, applications can be submitted only up to 12:30 PM.
context:
Corporate action › Buyback
Q: How to apply for  a buyback
A: You can apply for the Buyback through the Choice FinX Mobile Application or the Web Platform.
Through the Choice FinX Mobile Application:

Login to the Choice FinX application.
Go to the Portfolio section.
If you are eligible for the buyback and hold the respective stock, you will see a Buyback Banner below the portfolio valuation details.
Click on the banner and proceed with the buyback application as per the on-screen instructions.
Enter the quantity you wish to tender and submit your request.
Through the Choice FinX Web Platform:
1. Open the Choice FinX website:
https://finx.choiceindia.com/
2. Login using your registered Mobile Number/User ID and Password.
3. After successful login, you will be redirected to the Home Page.
4. Under the Smart Investments section, click on Buyback.
5. You will be able to view the list of stocks with active buyback opportunities.
6. Select the eligible stock and proceed with the application as per the on-screen instructions.

Important Points:

DDPI (Demat Debit and Pledge Instruction) must be active in your account to submit a buyback request.
If DDPI is not active, you can activate it by navigating to the Profile section of the FinX platform and completing the DDPI activation process.
The shares intended for buyback must be available in your Demat account and should not be pledged, frozen, or under any restriction at the time of application.
Buyback eligibility is available only to clients who held the shares on or before the specified record date.
If the stock is not visible under the Buyback section, it may indicate that the holdings do not meet the eligibility criteria.
On the last day of the buyback tendering period, applications can be submitted only up to 12:30 PM.

goldens.input: Process to modify the Address Details in offline mode?
goldens.expected_output:
To modify your bank details through the offline mode in Choice Broking, you'll need to follow these steps¹: 
    - Download the Account Modification Form: Visit Choice Broking's website and download the                 Account Modification Form. 
    - Fill in the Required Details: Fill out the form as per the updated Address Proof. 
    - Gather Required Documents: Collect necessary documents, including
      Compile photocopies: Collect photocopies of valid Proof of Address (POA) documents, such as utility bills, a passport, or a bank statement.
        Self-attest the documents: Sign the copies of your documents to self-attest them. 
Courier: Send the duly filled and signed modification form along with the photocopies of your bank proof documents to the Choice Broking office.
Branch visit: Submit the documents in person at a Choice Broking branch.
context:
Modification › How to delete or inactive a bank account
Q: Process to modify the Address Details in offline mode?
A: To modify your bank details through the offline mode in Choice Broking, you'll need to follow these steps¹: 
    - Download the Account Modification Form: Visit Choice Broking's website and download the                 Account Modification Form. 
    - Fill in the Required Details: Fill out the form as per the updated Address Proof. 
    - Gather Required Documents: Collect necessary documents, including
      Compile photocopies: Collect photocopies of valid Proof of Address (POA) documents, such as utility bills, a passport, or a bank statement.
        Self-attest the documents: Sign the copies of your documents to self-attest them. 
Courier: Send the duly filled and signed modification form along with the photocopies of your bank proof documents to the Choice Broking office.
Branch visit: Submit the documents in person at a Choice Broking branch.

goldens.input: What are the types of Buyback?
goldens.expected_output:
Buyback can be of two types - Tender Offer & Open Market Offer.

Tender Offer: Shareholders will have the option to submit (or tender) a portion or all of their shares. Within a certain time frame and at a premium to the current market price, this premium compensates investors for tendering their shares rather than holding on to them.

Market Offer: Companies buy back shares on the open market over an extended period of time.
context:
Orders › Basic Question Bank
Q: What are the types of Buyback?
A: Buyback can be of two types - Tender Offer & Open Market Offer.

Tender Offer: Shareholders will have the option to submit (or tender) a portion or all of their shares. Within a certain time frame and at a premium to the current market price, this premium compensates investors for tendering their shares rather than holding on to them.

Market Offer: Companies buy back shares on the open market over an extended period of time.

goldens.input: How Interest Charged by the Broker
goldens.expected_output:
•        Brokers charge interest on the funded amount (the money they provide to finance your purchase).
•        Rates vary broker to broker (usually 10–18% p.a.) depending on the broker and client profile.
•        Interest is calculated daily on the outstanding funded amount and debited to the client’s ledger at month-end or as per the broker’s cycle.
•        Example: If you use ₹1,00,000 broker funding at 21% p.a., then daily interest = 1,00,000 × 21% ÷ 365 ≈ ₹57.53 per day.
context:
MTF
Q: How Interest Charged by the Broker
A: •        Brokers charge interest on the funded amount (the money they provide to finance your purchase).
•        Rates vary broker to broker (usually 10–18% p.a.) depending on the broker and client profile.
•        Interest is calculated daily on the outstanding funded amount and debited to the client’s ledger at month-end or as per the broker’s cycle.
•        Example: If you use ₹1,00,000 broker funding at 21% p.a., then daily interest = 1,00,000 × 21% ÷ 365 ≈ ₹57.53 per day.

goldens.input: What documents are needed to open a LLP (Limited Liability Partnership) {As per LLP Act 2008) account?
goldens.expected_output:
1.        LLP pan card copy required (Note - Fill up form as per pan site name only)
2.        LLP address proof required (Note - Not more than two months old)
3.        LLP Bank proof required (Note – Personalize cheque copy or latest two month bank statement copy with IFSC and MICR code)
4.        LLP Designated Partners pan and address proof required with including their photograph, Designation and Contact numbers (As per MCA site)
5.        LLP Board resolution copy required on letterhead with Authorize signatory name, sign and stamp
6.        LLP Share Holding pattern required on letterhead including 10% and above shareholders (with CA attestation)
For individual shareholders --- Pan and address proof required.
For Non-individual shareholders --- Pan, address proof and shareholding pattern required
7.        LLP Ultimate Beneficial Owners (UBO) declaration copy required on letterhead
8.        LLP latest two years Annual reports (Balance sheet with P&L copy) required with CA’s attestation
9.        LLP CA’s Net-worth Certificate copy required on ca’s letterhead with CA’s stamp and signature. 
(Note - If LLP newly open and not complete one financial year)
10.        LLP Incorporation Certificate copy required which is issued by MCA (Ministry of Company Affair)
11.        LLP Agreement copy / Deed copy required
12.        Note - Verified with original and In-person verification stamp required on client all supporting proofs
13.        Note - Kindly fill up the client name in whole account opening form as per Pan Site name.
context:
Checklist of Account opening › Account opening
Q: What documents are needed to open a LLP (Limited Liability Partnership) {As per LLP Act 2008) account?
A: 1.        LLP pan card copy required (Note - Fill up form as per pan site name only)
2.        LLP address proof required (Note - Not more than two months old)
3.        LLP Bank proof required (Note – Personalize cheque copy or latest two month bank statement copy with IFSC and MICR code)
4.        LLP Designated Partners pan and address proof required with including their photograph, Designation and Contact numbers (As per MCA site)
5.        LLP Board resolution copy required on letterhead with Authorize signatory name, sign and stamp
6.        LLP Share Holding pattern required on letterhead including 10% and above shareholders (with CA attestation)
For individual shareholders --- Pan and address proof required.
For Non-individual shareholders --- Pan, address proof and shareholding pattern required
7.        LLP Ultimate Beneficial Owners (UBO) declaration copy required on letterhead
8.        LLP latest two years Annual reports (Balance sheet with P&L copy) required with CA’s attestation
9.        LLP CA’s Net-worth Certificate copy required on ca’s letterhead with CA’s stamp and signature. 
(Note - If LLP newly open and not complete one financial year)
10.        LLP Incorporation Certificate copy required which is issued by MCA (Ministry of Company Affair)
11.        LLP Agreement copy / Deed copy required
12.        Note - Verified with original and In-person verification stamp required on client all supporting proofs
13.        Note - Kindly fill up the client name in whole account opening form as per Pan Site name.

goldens.input: What is MTF (Margin Trading Facility)?
goldens.expected_output:
•        MTF is a facility offered by stockbrokers that allows clients to buy shares by paying only a part of the total value, and the rest is funded by the broker.
•        Example: If you want to buy shares worth ₹1,00,000 and your broker allows 4x leverage under MTF, you only need to pay ₹25,000, and the broker funds the remaining ₹75,000.
•        This helps clients take larger positions with limited own capital, but interest is charged on the funded portion.
context:
MTF
Q: What is MTF (Margin Trading Facility)?
A: •        MTF is a facility offered by stockbrokers that allows clients to buy shares by paying only a part of the total value, and the rest is funded by the broker.
•        Example: If you want to buy shares worth ₹1,00,000 and your broker allows 4x leverage under MTF, you only need to pay ₹25,000, and the broker funds the remaining ₹75,000.
•        This helps clients take larger positions with limited own capital, but interest is charged on the funded portion.

goldens.input: What Is a Demat Statement?
goldens.expected_output:
A Demat Statement is an official document that shows the holdings of securities in a client’s demat account. It reflects all shares, ETFs, bonds, mutual funds, and other securities held in electronic (dematerialized) form as of a specific date.

It is similar to a bank account statement, but instead of money, it shows securities and investments.
context:
Reports › Profit & loss
Q: What Is a Demat Statement?
A: A Demat Statement is an official document that shows the holdings of securities in a client’s demat account. It reflects all shares, ETFs, bonds, mutual funds, and other securities held in electronic (dematerialized) form as of a specific date.

It is similar to a bank account statement, but instead of money, it shows securities and investments.
